feedback_text:
DIAGNOSIS: The code has solid fundamentals but needs enhancement in four key areas. Most critical: remove the fallback_delimiter parameter to achieve true auto-detection (4.2 points). Second priority: implement structured error handling with custom exceptions (4.0 points). Third: expand edge case handling and data integrity reporting for more comprehensive anomaly detection.

DO NOT CHANGE (these sections are passing):
  ✓ code_idiomaticness: scored 100% - keep all current stdlib usage and avoid introducing any anti-patterns

REQUIRED FIXES (ordered by point impact):

  FIX 1: code_correctness.delimiter_detection (current: 50%, 4.2 pts at stake)
    Failed check: parameterized approach rather than auto-detection
    Expected: 1.0 score for auto-detection from data content
    Found: uses fallback_delimiter parameter, scoring as parameterized (0.5)
    → ACTION: Remove the fallback_delimiter parameter from the function signature. Make the delimiter detection fully automatic by having _detect_delimiter always return a detected delimiter (use ',' as internal fallback only when detection completely fails, not as a user parameter).

  FIX 2: code_robustness.error_handling (current: 50%, 4.0 pts at stake)
    Failed check: structured error reporting
    Expected: 1.0 score for structured error reporting
    Found: basic try/except with string messages
    → ACTION: Create a custom exception class (e.g., CSVParseError) with structured fields for error_type, row_number, and details. Replace generic Exception catching with specific exception types and raise the custom exception with structured data instead of just string messages.

  FIX 3: code_correctness.edge_case_resilience (current: 50%, 3.6 pts at stake)
    Failed check: percentage of edge cases handled without crash
    Expected: higher percentage of edge case coverage
    Found: handles basic edge cases but missing some scenarios
    → ACTION: Add handling for these additional edge cases: 1) files with only headers and no data rows, 2) files with inconsistent column counts across rows (pad shorter rows with empty strings), 3) files with BOM (byte order mark) at the beginning, 4) completely empty files (return empty data list with appropriate warning).

  FIX 4: code_robustness.data_integrity (current: 50%, 3.5 pts at stake)
    Failed check: reports all anomalies
    Expected: 1.0 score for comprehensive anomaly reporting
    Found: reports some anomalies but missing others
    → ACTION: Add detection and reporting for these data anomalies: 1) rows with significantly different column counts than the majority, 2) cells containing only whitespace (report as 'whitespace-only cells found'), 3) potential encoding issues (non-printable characters), 4) extremely long cells that might indicate parsing errors (e.g., >1000 characters).

  FIX 5: code_api_design.type_hints (current: 50%, 2.8 pts at stake)
    Failed check: complete type annotations
    Expected: 1.0 score for complete typing
    Found: partial type annotations missing on helper functions
    → ACTION: Add complete type hints to all helper functions: _get_content_string, _detect_delimiter, _parse_rows, _is_empty_row, _detect_headers, _looks_like_data, and _build_data_structure. Include parameter types, return types, and import necessary types from typing module.

  FIX 6: code_api_design.input_flexibility (current: 50%, 2.8 pts at stake)
    Failed check: multiple input types support
    Expected: 1.0 score for comprehensive input flexibility
    Found: handles basic input types but missing some
    → ACTION: Add support for these input types: 1) bytes input (detect encoding automatically), 2) list of strings (treat as pre-split lines), 3) URL strings starting with 'http' (fetch content), 4) add an optional 'encoding' parameter that defaults to 'utf-8' but allows override for the bytes input case.

  FIX 7: code_robustness.return_metadata (current: 50%, 2.5 pts at stake)
    Failed check: structured result object completeness
    Expected: 1.0 score for comprehensive structured result
    Found: basic ParseResult but missing some metadata
    → ACTION: Add these fields to ParseResult dataclass: 1) total_rows_processed: int, 2) column_count: int, 3) encoding_used: str, 4) parse_time_seconds: float (use time.time() to measure), 5) data_quality_score: float (calculated as percentage of clean rows without anomalies).

  FIX 8: code_api_design.docstring (current: 50%, 2.4 pts at stake)
    Failed check: complete docstring with comprehensive example
    Expected: 1.0 score for complete documentation
    Found: basic docstring missing some details
    → ACTION: Expand the docstring to include: 1) a 'Raises:' section documenting the custom exception types, 2) more detailed parameter descriptions explaining edge case behavior, 3) expand the example to show handling of the new structured exceptions and additional metadata fields in ParseResult.

  FIX 9: code_testability.pure_function (current: 50%, 2.4 pts at stake)
    Failed check: pure function design
    Expected: 1.0 score for completely pure function
    Found: minor side effects affecting purity
    → ACTION: Remove any remaining side effects: ensure the function doesn't print anything directly (move any print statements to the test section only), and if there are any logging calls, make them optional via a 'verbose' parameter that defaults to False.

  FIX 10: code_testability.modularity (current: 50%, 1.8 pts at stake)
    Failed check: sufficient helper function decomposition
    Expected: 1.0 score for 3+ focused helpers
    Found: has helpers but some could be more focused
    → ACTION: Split the largest helper functions into more focused units: 1) separate encoding detection into its own helper function, 2) create a dedicated function for column count normalization, 3) extract data quality scoring into its own helper function that can be independently tested.

  FIX 11: code_testability.test_examples (current: 50%, 1.8 pts at stake)
    Failed check: comprehensive test case coverage
    Expected: 1.0 score for complete test examples
    Found: basic test cases missing some scenarios
    → ACTION: Add test cases for the new functionality: 1) test the custom exception handling with malformed input, 2) test the new input types (bytes, list of strings), 3) test the additional metadata fields in ParseResult, 4) test the new edge cases (empty files, BOM handling, inconsistent column counts).
